v23EvalSummary.proofBoundary: ctxpack may be useful at lexical parity, but world-class claims require repeated lift on fixed corpora plus process-level context metrics.

releaseGate.decisionReason: Promote: every evaluated corpus beat lexical or reached a perfect lexical ceiling on non-test context recall, while maintaining validation-test recall under local-only proof thresholds.

environmentHealth.reason: Git history and object-content reads produced evaluable benchmark evidence.

notes: all-file recall trails lexical only after mixing validation targets into the file channel; context and validation channels are non-regressed
context channel reached the lexical ceiling with zero protected target misses
single-commit cold proof exceeded 5000ms but stayed under the cold-start diagnostic ceiling

environmentHealth.reason: Git history and object-content reads produced evaluable benchmark evidence.

notes: protected evidence miss rate at 10 is 0.319 overall, 0.083 for retrieval targets

environmentHealth.reason: Git history and object-content reads produced evaluable benchmark evidence.

notes: protected evidence miss rate at 10 is 0.429 overall, 0.000 for retrieval targets

environmentHealth.reason: Git history and object-content reads produced evaluable benchmark evidence.

notes: broad validation commands raise effective validation recall from 0.720 to 1.000
protected evidence miss rate at 10 is 0.475 overall, 0.286 for retrieval targets

limitations: Historical commit subjects are only proxies for real developer prompts.
No-context baseline is zero-file until editor anchor traces are available.
Token ROI is estimated from budget presets, not measured model billing.
Useful context at lexical parity is not world-class lift; repeated fixed-corpus lift and process-level context metrics are required.
helpsWhen: Tasks require choosing target files and tests across a repository.
Exact identifiers, related tests, history, and graph signals can reinforce each other.
Maintainers need source-free evidence for context quality over time.
doesNotHelpWhen: The task is a trivial single-file edit with an explicit path.
The right target file is absent from the safe local inventory.
The benchmark range contains low-information commit messages.
futureWork: v2.3: keep fixed corpus manifests and paired baseline verdicts current.
v2.4: add production semantic and precision backends only where eval gates show lift.
v2.5: ship only variants that beat lexical on every required corpus without unsafe privacy, runtime, or protected-evidence regressions.

benchmarkReport.description: Four-repo production-readiness proof using clean detached full fixtures for RefactoringMiner, ctxpack, ReAgent, and VeriSchema.
